acceptance_criteria.summary: bin/cra now defaults use_yolo to false and no longer forwards --yolo unless the wrapper receives explicit --yolo. Selecting the opt-in prints a visible yellow stderr warning containing WARNING and --yolo before exec'ing with --yolo. --no-yolo remains a backward-compatible no-op.

tests/test_cra_launcher_defaults.py covers the safe default (plain invocation omits --yolo from captured argv) and the explicit opt-in path (--yolo present in captured argv and a warning present on captured stderr), following the mock-capture-binary pattern from tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py.

commits.message: test(CL-v1tb): red — cra safe yolo-default regressions
feat(CL-v1tb): green — cra yolo default requires explicit opt-in

tests.result: Red gate before production change failed as expected: 2 failed. Default argv still contained --yolo, and the explicit --yolo path forwarded --yolo twice with no way to distinguish opt-in behavior.

tests.command: uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_cra_launcher_defaults.py tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py -v
tests.result: Green gate after production change passed: 12 passed (2 cra tests + 10 cld regression tests, no regressions).
Passed with no shell syntax errors.

downstream_impacts: bin/cra no longer forwards --yolo to the Cursor Agent binary by default for plain invocations.
Users who intentionally pass --yolo still get --yolo forwarded, but now receive a visible stderr warning first.
--no-yolo is now a no-op (yolo is already off by default) but remains accepted and consumed for backward compatibility with existing invocations.
No changes to bin/cld, bin/cdx, or bead-context/envelope work; those remain out of scope per sibling beads.

evidence_refs.description: The red pytest run against tests/test_cra_launcher_defaults.py failed before production code changed because default cra argv still included --yolo unconditionally and there was no explicit-opt-in flag to distinguish or warn on.
The green pytest run passed after bin/cra changed use_yolo's default to false, added an explicit --yolo opt-in case, and added the stderr warning before forwarding --yolo.

tests/test_cra_launcher_defaults.py::test_cra_defaults_to_no_yolo_without_explicit_opt_in and ::test_cra_yolo_explicit_opt_in_forwards_flag_and_warns cover the safe default and explicit opt-in paths using a mock agent-capture binary on PATH, following the tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py subprocess-mock pattern.
uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_cra_launcher_defaults.py tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py returned 12 passed with no regressions in the sibling cld launcher test suite.
